name: CosyVoice
tagline: Multilingual LLM-based text-to-speech with zero-shot voice cloning and streaming output
seoTitle: CosyVoice: Open-Source Multilingual TTS & Voice Cloning
metaDescription: CosyVoice is an open-source, LLM-based text-to-speech engine with zero-shot voice cloning, 9-language coverage, streaming output, and instruction-controlled speech.
overview: CosyVoice is an open-source text-to-speech (TTS) system built on a large language model. From the FunAudioLLM team, it turns written text into natural-sounding speech and can clone a voice from a short reference clip without any per-speaker fine-tuning (zero-shot cloning). The project ships pretrained models, plus full inference, training, and deployment code.

It is designed for multilingual use. The latest Fun-CosyVoice 3.0 covers 9 common languages (Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, German, Spanish, French, Italian, and Russian) along with 18+ Chinese dialects and accents, and supports cross-lingual voice cloning. It also accepts instructions to control language, dialect, emotion, speed, and volume.

CosyVoice supports bi-directional streaming: text can stream in while audio streams out, with latency reported as low as 150ms. It includes built-in text normalization (reading numbers and special symbols) and pronunciation inpainting for Chinese Pinyin and English CMU phonemes, giving finer control for production use. You can run it from a script, a Gradio web demo, or as a deployed gRPC/FastAPI service.
features: Zero-shot voice cloning: copy a target voice from a short reference sample, including cross-lingual cloning, with no per-speaker training
Multilingual coverage: 9 common languages plus 18+ Chinese dialects and accents in the Fun-CosyVoice 3.0 model
Bi-directional streaming: text-in and audio-out streaming with latency reported as low as 150ms
Instruction control: adjust language, dialect, emotion, speed, and volume via instructions
Built-in text normalization for numbers and symbols, plus pronunciation inpainting for Pinyin and CMU phonemes
Multiple run modes: Python script, Gradio web demo, and gRPC/FastAPI deployment, with optional vLLM and TensorRT-LLM acceleration

gettingStarted.intro: CosyVoice runs in a Conda environment. You clone the repo with its submodules, install the Python requirements, download a pretrained model, then run the example script. The team recommends the Fun-CosyVoice3-0.5B model for best quality.

steps.heading: Clone the repository with submodules
steps.body: Clone the repo recursively so the bundled submodules come along. If the submodule clone fails due to network issues, re-run the update command until it succeeds.
steps.code:
git clone --recursive https://github.com/FunAudioLLM/CosyVoice.git
cd CosyVoice
git submodule update --init --recursive

steps.heading: Create the Conda environment and install
steps.body: Create a Python 3.10 Conda environment, activate it, and install the requirements.
steps.code:
conda create -n cosyvoice -y python=3.10
conda activate cosyvoice
pip install -r requirements.txt

steps.heading: Download a pretrained model
steps.body: Download a pretrained model into the pretrained_models folder. You can use ModelScope or, for users outside China, the Hugging Face Hub.

steps.heading: Run the example or web demo
steps.body: Run example.py to generate speech, or launch the Gradio web demo to try it interactively in the browser.
steps.code:
python example.py
# or launch the web demo
python3 webui.py --port 50000 --model_dir pretrained_models/CosyVoice-300M

useCases: Cloning a specific voice from a short audio sample to narrate text in the same voice, including across languages
Building multilingual voice assistants or readers that speak Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, and other supported languages
Adding low-latency streaming speech output to real-time apps where text is generated and spoken on the fly
Generating expressive speech with controlled emotion, speed, dialect, or volume for media, dubbing, or accessibility tools
Deploying a self-hosted TTS service via gRPC or FastAPI, with optional vLLM or TensorRT-LLM acceleration for throughput
